_note: Blueprint registry — the authoring/generator source for every shape the kernel does not dispatch on (kernel-owned categories/primitives live in form-ontology.json). Generated from code by scripts/scan_form_blueprints.py --emit-registry, then curated. Each row carries the full NodeID (pkg, level, type, inst). Current direct-source form-ontology-loader.fk admits only a reviewed bootstrap subset; the full registry still needs a Form-owned runtime data path. To add a shape: add a row here, then route usage through the reviewed bootstrap set or a later Form-owned registry image rather than raw numbers.

blueprints.name: UE-MATH-INC
blueprints.meaning: increment (++) prefix or postfix

blueprints.name: UE-MATH-DEC
blueprints.meaning: decrement (--) prefix or postfix

blueprints.name: UE-LOOP
blueprints.meaning: universal loop (for/while/foreach): init?, cond?, update?, body

blueprints.name: UE-SWITCH
blueprints.meaning: universal switch/select: scrutinee + case clauses

blueprints.name: UE-CASE
blueprints.meaning: switch case clause: match values + body

blueprints.name: UE-TRY
blueprints.meaning: try block + catch clauses

blueprints.name: UE-CATCH
blueprints.meaning: catch clause: typed param + body

blueprints.name: UE-THROW
blueprints.meaning: throw/raise an expression

blueprints.name: UE-CLASS
blueprints.meaning: class declaration: name + type-params? + bases? + members

blueprints.name: UE-INTERFACE
blueprints.meaning: interface declaration: name + bases? + signatures

blueprints.name: DOC-DOC
blueprints.meaning: root document

blueprints.name: DOC-HEADING
blueprints.meaning: (level, text)

blueprints.name: DOC-PARAGRAPH
blueprints.meaning: (text)

blueprints.name: DOC-CODE-BLOCK
blueprints.meaning: (lang, content)

blueprints.name: DOC-BLANK
blueprints.meaning: blank-line marker (discarded post-parse)

blueprints.name: CHANNEL-BREATH-GIFT
blueprints.meaning: debt-free channel offering: gift, release condition, consent/freedom, and boundary

blueprints.name: CHANNEL-RESONANCE-RECEIPT
blueprints.meaning: relation evidence receipt: observer, other, gift, coherence delta, disturbance, debt, freedom, and next contact

blueprints.name: BML-COMPILER-IMAGE
blueprints.meaning: Serialized BML compiler source image artifact

blueprints.name: JSON-OBJECT
blueprints.meaning: map: list of (key, value) pairs

blueprints.name: JSON-ARRAY
blueprints.meaning: sequence of values

blueprints.name: JSON-PAIR
blueprints.meaning: (key-string, value)

blueprints.name: JSON-NULL
blueprints.meaning: null literal

blueprints.name: KERNEL-CORE-IMAGE
blueprints.meaning: Serialized minimal kernel-core self image: source path, image path, core name, primitive count, dispatch count, proof count, and executable witness recipes

blueprints.name: HOST-KERNEL-INTERFACE-IMAGE
blueprints.meaning: Serialized host-kernel-interface image: organ/world-port/carrier/primitive/fkwu-gap counts and witness recipes binding resource-port and storage-port contracts

blueprints.name: SOVEREIGN-BOUNDARY-RECEIPT
blueprints.meaning: Generic boundary decision receipt carrying the selected allow/stop/witness/re-enter choice and its traceable CHOICE-RECEIPT.

blueprints.name: SOVEREIGN-BOUNDARY-EVIDENCE
blueprints.meaning: Generic boundary evidence: subject, offered interface, protocol, reach, consent, violation, repair evidence, vitality, certainty, and time for allow/stop/witness/re-entry decisions.

blueprints.name: KERNEL-SATSANG-PART
blueprints.meaning: One self-describing kernel part as an interned native node: name, recipe body, verification recipe, and the expected outside value any sibling kernel can attest through the satsang circle.

blueprints.name: KERNEL-SATSANG-IMAGE
blueprints.meaning: A whole self-describing kernel as one persistable node: kernel name plus its KERNEL-SATSANG-PART children, the .fkb-round-trippable binary shape the kernel shrinks to and expands back from.

blueprints.name: YAML-DOC
blueprints.meaning: root document = sequence of pairs / list items

blueprints.name: YAML-PAIR
blueprints.meaning: (key-string, value)

blueprints.name: YAML-ITEM
blueprints.meaning: list-item value

blueprints.name: YAML-LIST
blueprints.meaning: (sequence of items)

blueprints.name: PRIM-REGISTRY-PROBE
blueprints.meaning: Probe blueprint for primitive-registry.fk record/method verifications — the registry's own record type, used by record_new/record_get/method_define entries

blueprints.name: STATIC-DYNAMIC-LANE
blueprints.meaning: Aggregate lane that proves static memory has live dynamic successors.

blueprints.name: STATIC-DYNAMIC-SURFACE
blueprints.meaning: Static source surface projected into a dynamic successor cell with proof and release state.

blueprints.name: STATIC-DYNAMIC-SUCCESSOR
blueprints.meaning: Dynamic successor cell named by a static source surface.

blueprints.name: WORLD-MODEL-GROWTH
blueprints.meaning: World-model growth receipt root: sensed readings digested into blueprint, recipe, and named-cell parts, embodied only after trusted-core follow.

blueprints.name: FORM-LIST
blueprints.meaning: an S-expression list

blueprints.meaning: (name)

blueprints.name: PYC-NAME-LIST
blueprints.meaning: list of name strings

blueprints.name: PYC-DEF-FULL
blueprints.meaning: (name, params, ret-type)

blueprints.name: PYC-PARAM-LIST

blueprints.name: MS-PAIR
blueprints.meaning: minimal-surface: the intern door's two-child composition (m4a of hati-os.form)

blueprints.name: VALUE-NONE
blueprints.meaning: Structural null/None sentinel used by the fourth-arm flatten/runtime path where host VNull is represented as a content-addressed blueprint node.

blueprints.name: FSH-SCRIPT
blueprints.meaning: Form shell lowered-script cell marker used by shell-lower.fk when wrapping an fsh source recipe as a CELL-V0.

blueprints.name: CHANNEL-OSI-LAYER
blueprints.meaning: one native channel protocol layer: osi index, name, phase, carrier, policy, recipe

blueprints.name: CHANNEL-FLOW
blueprints.meaning: native channel protocol flow: carrier, protocol, seven OSI layer cells, channel policy

blueprints.name: CELL-CIRCLE
blueprints.meaning: consentful group container: members, shared context, interface, discovery, confidentiality, export policy, carrier flow

blueprints.name: CIRCLE-OFFER
blueprints.meaning: discoverable circle offering: circle id, offered-by cell, title, discovery policy, invitation policy, boundary

blueprints.name: CIRCLE-INVITATION
blueprints.meaning: circle join invitation: circle id, inviter, invitee, invitation, boundary, expiry

blueprints.name: CIRCLE-SHARE
blueprints.meaning: owned circle share: circle id, author, kind, payload, visibility, consent policy

blueprints.name: CIRCLE-CONSENSUS
blueprints.meaning: circle consensus call: circle id, subject, voters, approvals, threshold, decision

blueprints.name: CIRCLE-REFUSAL
blueprints.meaning: consensus-backed circle refusal when invasion is observed

blueprints.name: SHAMBALLA-CRYSTAL
blueprints.meaning: the union-of-forces dyad of the Shamballa Crystal (code 4): two cells composed in balance — content-addressed co-creation

blueprints.name: GEMATRIA-WORD
blueprints.meaning: a Hebrew word as a cell — its letters composed; its content-address is its gematria value, so two differently-spelled words of equal value share one address (echad=ahava=13)

blueprints.name: SANSKRIT-DERIVE
blueprints.meaning: a Sanskrit derivation recipe — (root, affix) composed into a word-cell; recursive (a derived cell can be the root of another), the generative grammar made executable; the root is recoverable as the first child

blueprints.name: CIRCLE-EXPORT-CONSENT
blueprints.meaning: explicit consent for evidence leaving a circle: recipient, fidelity, purpose, expiry, consensus

blueprints.name: CIRCLE-RECEIPT
blueprints.meaning: circle integration/refusal/export receipt: circle id, member, subject, action, reason, cost

blueprints.name: SATSANG-SILENCE
blueprints.meaning: circle silence event oriented toward truth and shared being

blueprints.name: SATSANG-INQUIRY
blueprints.meaning: truth-oriented inquiry offered inside a circle

blueprints.name: SATSANG-POINTING
blueprints.meaning: non-command pointing offered inside a satsang circle

blueprints.name: NATIVE-ROUTE-OBS
blueprints.meaning: one observed API route sample: path, method, calls, latency, errors, bytes, native state, grammar, handler, user flow, and north-star fit

blueprints.name: NATIVE-ROUTE-ATTENTION
blueprints.meaning: traffic-weighted route pressure and next action for native front-door promotion

blueprints.name: NATIVE-ROUTE-GOAL
blueprints.meaning: native-route north-star state: target share, observed routes, attentions, and next chosen movement

blueprints.name: CHOICE-CANDIDATE
blueprints.meaning: one branch candidate for a choice receipt: path, category, eligibility, pressure, score, and evidence

blueprints.name: CHOICE-TRACE
blueprints.meaning: choice witness counters: attempts, successes, failures, and silences

blueprints.name: CHOICE-VALUE
blueprints.meaning: choice value surface keeping branch-prediction feedback distinct from alignment, knowing, and trust

blueprints.name: CHOICE-RECEIPT
blueprints.meaning: trace-preserving choice receipt: expression, surface, category, candidates, selected path, outcome, certainty, value, why/who/where/when, cost, and trace

blueprints.name: HANDLER-NOT-FOUND
blueprints.meaning: Sentinel NodeID the verb-router returns when no handler matches a name. Absence, not a shape.

blueprints.name: CHOICE-SIGNATURE
blueprints.meaning: compressed choice receipt signature preserving category, selected path, outcome, certainty bucket, value buckets, witness, coordinate, and trace counters

blueprints.name: BOOT-QUERY
blueprints.meaning: a question offered on a booted body's input channel — children (domain, name), the two keys lookup-cell resolves; the same act as an extension offer (axiom-5), distinguished only by category

blueprints.name: HOLO-ONE
blueprints.meaning: The One — the prima materia, the single source Blueprint all holographic forms differentiate from (the ground every bulk is interned under). Carries its own address, distinct from HANDLER-NOT-FOUND: the source of all forms is not absence.

blueprints.name: OFFER-V0
blueprints.meaning: axiom-5 offer in flight: substrate-ref of the offered cell + 0..many arg cells; the content-addressed NodeID is the correlation token

blueprints.name: OFFER-ACK
blueprints.meaning: wire acknowledgment of one offer: correlation token + exactly one of the four ack values (nothing/0/1/node)

blueprints.name: ACK-NOTHING
blueprints.meaning: silence reified as a node on the wire — an ack value distinct from the absence of any ack

blueprints.name: ACK-NODE
blueprints.meaning: counter-offer ack value wrapping a cell — the one ack value that recurs; threading follows it to a terminal

blueprints.name: INSTALL-IFACE
blueprints.meaning: the offered interface of an installable callable — child 0 the arity as a trivial-int; part of the artifact's own composition so it cannot be claimed apart from what the artifact is

blueprints.name: INSTALL-ARTIFACT
blueprints.meaning: a compiled capability as a cell — children (INSTALL-IFACE, body recipe); content-addressing makes the interface inseparable from the identity (axiom-3)

blueprints.name: INSTALL-OFFER
blueprints.meaning: an install offer — children (name trivial-string, INSTALL-ARTIFACT, expected INSTALL-IFACE); the kernel acknowledges by binding the name or acknowledges 0 (refusal)

blueprints.name: INSTALLED-LEAF
blueprints.meaning: one binding in a callable table — children (name trivial-string, INSTALL-ARTIFACT); the table grows by offer, never rebinds (first-bind-wins)

blueprints.name: UUID
blueprints.meaning: the arrival event/context

blueprints.name: ARRIVAL-QUALITY
blueprints.meaning: felt quality of this arrival

blueprints.name: ARRIVAL-INQUIRY
blueprints.meaning: an inquiry offered at arrival

blueprints.name: ARRIVAL-RESONANCE
blueprints.meaning: what the field notices in the arrival

blueprints.name: ARRIVAL-OBS
blueprints.meaning: arrival-flavored observation (structurally parallel to THIA-OBS 1805)

blueprints.name: CELL-IDENTITY
blueprints.meaning: sovereign identity a cell presents

blueprints.name: CONTACT-THREAD
blueprints.meaning: logical relationship between two identities

blueprints.name: AO-OFFER
blueprints.meaning: An unsolicited afferent offer: a world-event as a cell — (line, at-tick, payload). Signals and interrupts are two readings of this one shape.

blueprints.name: AO-ENTRY
blueprints.meaning: One handler-table row: line ↦ handler-recipe name (signal number or IRQ line to handler, as data).

blueprints.name: AO-TABLE
blueprints.meaning: The handler table as a cell — introspectable, shippable dispatch surface for afferent offers.

blueprints.name: AO-MASK
blueprints.meaning: The boundary's currently-closed doors — sigprocmask and the interrupt controller's mask register as one shape.

blueprints.name: AO-DISPATCH
blueprints.meaning: The node-acknowledgment of an afferent offer: which handler fires, answering which offer (correlation by content).

blueprints.name: AO-NOTHING
blueprints.meaning: The nothing-acknowledgment as a visible cell: no event in the window — timeout==nothing, shared by the signal and interrupt readings.

blueprints.name: OAC-NOTHING
blueprints.meaning: The nothing-acknowledgment arm of the offer/ack core: silence / decline / timeout — first-class, the ground every fail and stop resolves to (axiom-1, axiom-5).

blueprints.name: OAC-ZERO
blueprints.meaning: The 0-acknowledgment arm of the offer/ack core: received-no / false / not-this (axiom-5).

blueprints.name: OAC-ONE
blueprints.meaning: The 1-acknowledgment arm of the offer/ack core: received the expected result, carrying its payload cell (axiom-5).

blueprints.name: OAC-NODE
blueprints.meaning: The node-acknowledgment arm of the offer/ack core: a counter-offer cell back, the one ack that re-enters the offer (axiom-5).

blueprints.name: BML-CTOR-ENV
blueprints.meaning: Blueprint for a BML constructor's environment record — the record a constructor body executes against, holding its named fields (hSyntax, strName, hArgs, …). Returned by bml-constructor-env-blueprint in grammars/bml.fk.

blueprints.name: BML-CONSTRUCTOR-INSTANCE
blueprints.meaning: generic BML constructor record for source-defined runtime class instances

blueprints.name: BML-PARSE-STACK-CONSTRUCTOR
blueprints.meaning: BML ParseStack constructor record for source-defined parse stack instances

blueprints.name: BML-SOURCE-DECLARATION
blueprints.meaning: BML compiler source declaration runtime record

blueprints.name: BML-SOURCE-CARRIER
blueprints.meaning: BML compiler source carrier runtime record

blueprints.name: BML-PARSED-SOURCE-PARSER
blueprints.meaning: BML parsed source parser adapter runtime record

blueprints.name: BML-COMPILER-DECLARATION-LOWERING-STAGE
blueprints.meaning: BML compiler declaration lowering stage runtime record

blueprints.name: BML-COMPILER-SOURCE-LOWERING-FLOW
blueprints.meaning: BML compiler source lowering flow runtime record

blueprints.name: BML-MUTABLE-LOCAL-FRAME
blueprints.meaning: BML-native mutable local frame record: source receipt plus mutable named slots for declare/assign/read lowering.

blueprints.name: CLASS-BP
blueprints.meaning: Blueprint for a Python class value under the BMF evaluator — a record holding each method as a field; the captured def-site env.

blueprints.name: INSTANCE-BP
blueprints.meaning: Blueprint for a Python instance value under the BMF evaluator — a record holding data fields plus a __class__ pointer.

blueprints.name: SUPER-BP
blueprints.meaning: Blueprint for a super() proxy under the BMF evaluator — a sentinel-tagged record carrying (__self__, __start_class__); start-class is the __base__ of the class that defined the executing method, so super().M() resolves M from there with the original self.

blueprints.name: SHAMBALLA-RAISED
blueprints.meaning: the regal envelope of Ho Ka O iLi iLi (code 3): a cell composed under a higher-level blueprint — assemblage point lifted

blueprints.name: PROGRAM-IMAGE-RECIPE-ENVELOPE
blueprints.meaning: Program-image recipe carrier envelope node.

blueprints.name: PROGRAM-IMAGE-RECIPE-TABLE
blueprints.meaning: Program-image recipe carrier table section node.

blueprints.name: PROGRAM-IMAGE-RECIPE-FN-ROOTS
blueprints.meaning: Program-image recipe carrier function-root collection node.

blueprints.name: PROGRAM-IMAGE-RECIPE-NODE-ROWS
blueprints.meaning: Program-image recipe carrier node-row collection node.

blueprints.name: PROGRAM-IMAGE-RECIPE-NODE-ROW
blueprints.meaning: Program-image recipe carrier node-row node with four integer children.

blueprints.name: PROGRAM-IMAGE-RECIPE-STRING-ROWS
blueprints.meaning: Program-image recipe carrier string-byte-row collection node.

blueprints.name: PROGRAM-IMAGE-RECIPE-STRING-ROW
blueprints.meaning: Program-image recipe carrier byte row node with integer byte children.

blueprints.name: TYPED-LITERAL-INT
blueprints.meaning: Typed literal int wrapper node carrying one trivial int child.

blueprints.name: TYPED-LITERAL-STRING
blueprints.meaning: Typed literal string wrapper node carrying one trivial string child.

blueprints.name: TYPED-LITERAL-LIST
blueprints.meaning: Typed literal list wrapper node carrying typed literal children.

blueprints.name: PROGRAM-IMAGE-TYPED-ENVELOPE
blueprints.meaning: Typed program-image envelope node carrying typed literal metadata and typed table.

blueprints.name: PROGRAM-IMAGE-TYPED-TABLE
blueprints.meaning: Typed program-image table section node.

blueprints.name: PROGRAM-IMAGE-TYPED-FN-ROOTS
blueprints.meaning: Typed program-image function-root collection node.

blueprints.name: PROGRAM-IMAGE-TYPED-NODE-ROWS
blueprints.meaning: Typed program-image node-row collection node.

blueprints.name: PROGRAM-IMAGE-TYPED-NODE-ROW
blueprints.meaning: Typed program-image node-row node with four typed int children.

blueprints.name: PROGRAM-IMAGE-TYPED-STRING-ROWS
blueprints.meaning: Typed program-image string-byte-row collection node.

blueprints.name: PROGRAM-IMAGE-TYPED-STRING-ROW
blueprints.meaning: Typed program-image string-byte row node with typed int byte children.

blueprints.name: PROGRAM-IMAGE-RECIPE-SYMBOL-IMAGE
blueprints.meaning: Program-image recipe carrier symbol image node.

blueprints.name: PROGRAM-IMAGE-RECIPE-SYMBOL-ROWS
blueprints.meaning: Program-image recipe carrier symbol-row collection node.

blueprints.name: PROGRAM-IMAGE-RECIPE-SYMBOL-ROW
blueprints.meaning: Program-image recipe carrier canonical symbol row node.

blueprints.name: PROGRAM-IMAGE-RECIPE-NODE-SYMBOL-ROWS
blueprints.meaning: Program-image recipe carrier node-symbol-row collection node.

blueprints.name: PROGRAM-IMAGE-RECIPE-NODE-SYMBOL-ROW
blueprints.meaning: Program-image recipe carrier node symbol row with dependency targets.

blueprints.name: PROGRAM-IMAGE-RECIPE-SYMBOL-DEPENDENCY-TARGETS
blueprints.meaning: Program-image recipe carrier symbol dependency target collection node.

blueprints.name: PROGRAM-IMAGE-RECIPE-SYMBOL-DEPENDENCY-TARGET
blueprints.meaning: Program-image recipe carrier dependency symbol and target-node edge.
